topic: later harvest, grape ripeness, and wine body
trigger_keywords: vino tinto elaborado con vendimia tardía

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Healthy red grapes remain on the vine longer before harvest under conditions that still permit ripening.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Additional hang time usually allows further sugar accumulation and flavour development and may reduce berry water, while acidity tends to decline.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: After fermentation, the wine may show riper fruit flavours, higher alcohol and fuller body, but the result depends on fruit health, weather, yield, extraction, and winemaking.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Later harvesting can produce riper flavours and fuller body through greater sugar and flavour maturity; it does not guarantee concentration or quality.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
